 {"id":"gt-9j9","title":"CLI: worker status reporting commands","description":"Worker status reporting CLI for polecats to report progress.

## Commands

### gt worker started
```
gt worker started <issue-id> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports work started on issue.

### gt worker progress
```
gt worker progress <issue-id> <0-100> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports percentage complete.

### gt worker blocked
```
gt worker blocked <issue-id> <reason> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports blocked status with reason.

### gt worker completed
```
gt worker completed <issue-id> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports task completion.

### gt worker failed
```
gt worker failed <issue-id> <reason> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports task failure.

## Implementation
Each command sends mail to refinery with structured content:
```go
type WorkerStatusReport struct {
    IssueID    string
    Status     string  // started|progress|blocked|completed|failed
    Progress   int     // 0-100 for progress
    Reason     string  // for blocked/failed
    Message    string  // optional detail
    ReportedAt time.Time
}
```

## Message Format
Subject: "[STATUS] <issue-id>: <status>"
Body: JSON-encoded WorkerStatusReport

## Default Recipient
```go
// Determine from context
func getDefaultRecipient() string {
    rig := os.Getenv("GT_RIG")
    if rig != "" {
        return rig + "/refinery"
    }
    return "refinery/"
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/worker.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/worker_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 commands implemented
- [ ] Status sent as mail to refinery
- [ ] Structured JSON body for parsing
- [ ] Works from polecat session context","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:52.795695-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:26.715967-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-e9k","title":"Workspace cleanup: preflight and postflight","description":"Workspace preflight and postflight commands for clean state management.

## Preflight
```
gt preflight [--rig <rig>] [--dry-run]
```

Run before starting batch work:
1. Clean stale mail in inboxes
2. Check for stuck workers (warn)
3. Check rig health (polecats, refinery)
4. Verify git state is clean
5. Run bd sync to ensure beads current

## Postflight
```
gt postflight [--rig <rig>] [--archive-mail] [--dry-run]
```

Run after batch work completes:
1. Archive old mail with --archive-mail
2. Clean up stale integration branches
3. Sync beads
4. Report on rig state

## Implementation
```go
func Preflight(rigName string, dryRun bool) (*PreflightReport, error)
func Postflight(rigName string, opts PostflightOptions) (*PostflightReport, error)
```

## Report Structures
```go
type PreflightReport struct {
    MailCleaned    int
    RigHealthy     bool
    StuckWorkers   []string
    Warnings       []string
}

type PostflightReport struct {
    MailArchived   int
    BranchesCleaned int
    Warnings       []string
}
```

## Note

These are workspace maintenance commands, not tied to "swarm" lifecycle. Run them anytime to keep the rig clean.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Preflight cleans stale state
- [ ] Postflight archives old mail
- [ ] Both have --dry-run mode
- [ ] Clear reports of actions taken","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:15.997677-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:01.55269-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-ih0s","title":"Fix blocking bugs (gt-dsfi, gt-n7z7, gm-c6b)","description":"Fix bugs blocking Witness functionality:

1. gt-dsfi: handoff deadlock
   - Polecats hang when trying to exit
   - Blocks shutdown request handler

2. gt-n7z7: refinery foreground race condition  
   - Sometimes detects parent as already running
   - Blocks reliable Refinery startup

3. gm-c6b: mail coordination
   - Cross-rig mail should use town-level database
   - Affects Witness <-> Mayor communication

These should be fixed early as they'll block integration testing.","notes":"Fixed gt-dsfi and gt-n7z7. Issue gm-c6b not found in database.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:25.803822-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.789714-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ih0s","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.430142-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.789714-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-ihzqr","title":"Formula SDK: Developer Tooling for Workflow Authors","description":"SDK for creating, testing, debugging, and validating formulas.

## Components

### Forge (Creation)
```bash
mol forge new my-workflow        # Scaffold new formula
mol forge extend base-workflow   # Create variant
mol forge lint my-workflow       # Check syntax/style
```

### Lab (Testing)
```bash
mol lab run my-workflow --dry-run   # Mock execution
mol lab test my-workflow            # Run formula tests
mol lab trace my-workflow           # Step-through debug
```

### Validator (Verification)
```bash
mol validate my-workflow                    # Check well-formedness
mol validate my-workflow --protocol=Reviewable  # Check protocol compliance
mol validate my-workflow --schematic=shiny  # Check schematic compatibility
```

### Analyzer (Static Analysis)
```bash
mol analyze my-workflow --deps     # Dependency graph
mol analyze my-workflow --coverage # Step coverage
mol analyze my-workflow --cycles   # Detect cycles
```

## Relationship to Other Concepts

The SDK operates on:
- Formulas (source level)
- Protos (compiled level)
- Molecules (runtime level)

And validates against:
- Protocols (type system)
- Schematics (domain composition)

## Open Questions

1. Bundled vs separate - mol subcommands or formula-kit binary?
2. IDE integration - LSP for formula files?
3. Test harness - how to mock steps? fixtures?

## Related

- docs/formula_evolution.md - "SDK Question" section
- gt-8tmz.30 - Proto debugging tools (subset)
- gt-8tmz.31 - Formula validation (subset)

 {"id":"gt-iib","title":"Architecture: Decentralized rig structure with per-rig agents","description":"## Decision

Adopt decentralized architecture where each rig contains all its agents (mayor/, witness/, refinery/, polecats/) rather than centralizing mayor clones at town level.

## Town Level Structure

```
~/ai/                              # Town root
├── config/                        # Town config (VISIBLE, not hidden)
│   ├── town.json                  # {"type": "town"}
│   ├── rigs.json                  # Registry of managed rigs
│   └── federation.json            # Wasteland config (future)
│
├── mayor/                         # Mayor's HOME at town level
│   ├── CLAUDE.md
│   ├── mail/inbox.jsonl
│   └── state.json
│
└── <rigs>/                        # Managed projects
```

## Rig Level Structure (e.g., wyvern)

```
wyvern/                            # Rig = clone of project repo
├── .git/info/exclude              # Gas Town adds: polecats/ refinery/ witness/ mayor/
├── .beads/                        # Beads (if project uses it)
├── [project files]                # Clean project code on main
│
├── polecats/                      # Worker clones
│   └── <name>/                    # Each is a git clone
│
├── refinery/
│   ├── rig/                       # Refinery's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
├── witness/                       # NEW: Per-rig pit boss
│   ├── rig/                       # Witness's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
└── mayor/
    ├── rig/                       # Mayor's clone for this rig
    └── state.json
```

## Key Decisions

1. **Visible config dir**: `config/` not `.gastown/` (models don't find hidden dirs)
2. **Witness per-rig**: Each rig has its own Witness (pit boss) with its own clone
3. **Mayor decentralized**: Mayor's clones live IN each rig at `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
4. **Minimal invasiveness**: Only `.git/info/exclude` modified, no commits to project
5. **Clone subdir name**: Keep `rig/` for consistency (refinery/rig/, witness/rig/, mayor/rig/)

## Role Detection

- Town root or mayor/ → Mayor (town level)
- Rig root → Mayor (canonical main)
- <rig>/mayor/rig/ → Mayor (rig-specific)
- <rig>/refinery/rig/ → Refinery
- <rig>/witness/rig/ → Witness
- <rig>/polecats/<name>/ → Polecat

## Migration from PGT

- `mayor/rigs/<rig>/` → `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
- `<rig>/town/` → eliminated (rig root IS the clone)
- Add `witness/` to each rig","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:19.913928-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-iib","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.374551-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-lek6","title":"gt rig reset --stale: Clear orphaned in_progress items","description":"Reset in_progress issues when their assigned agent no longer exists.

## Problem
When polecats die without cleanup, their issues remain in_progress forever.
Need a way to bulk-reset these orphaned items.

## Command
```bash
gt rig reset --stale [--dry-run]
```

## Logic
For each in_progress issue in rig:
1. Parse assignee (e.g., "gastown/furiosa")
2. Map to tmux session name (gt-gastown-furiosa)
3. If session does NOT exist:
   - Reset status to "open"
   - Clear assignee
4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)
   OR check if crew tmux session exists

## Output
```
Resetting stale work in gastown:
  gt-abc: gastown/furiosa (no session) → open
  gt-def: gastown/nux (no session) → open
  Skipped: gt-xyz: gastown/crew/max (persistent)
Reset 2 issues, skipped 1
```

## Related
- gt-2kz: CLI cleanup commands for stale state
- gt-rdmw: orphan-check in deacon patrol
- gt-orphans command (list orphaned molecules)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:46.962413-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.383925-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.383925-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

 {"id":"gt-lexye","title":"Create internal/tui/ package with feed model","description":"Create the TUI infrastructure for gt feed.

## Package structure
```
internal/tui/
  feed/
    model.go       # bubbletea Model struct
    view.go        # View() rendering
    update.go      # Update() message handling
    events.go      # Event stream subscription (tail .feed.jsonl)
    styles.go      # lipgloss styles
    keys.go        # Key bindings
```

## Model components
1. **AgentTree** - Collapsible tree of agents by role
2. **EventStream** - Scrollable viewport of recent events
3. **StatusBar** - Current filter, help hints
4. **FilterPopup** - Modal for setting filters

## Key bindings
- j/k - scroll events
- J/K - scroll agent tree
- tab - switch focus (tree/stream)
- enter - expand/details
- / - search
- f - filter popup
- q - quit
- ? - help

## Event subscription
- Tail ~/gt/.feed.jsonl using fsnotify or polling
- Parse JSONL events
- Send as bubbletea messages to update model","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:13:40.29527-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-28T16:21:50.5417-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T16:21:50.5417-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-lexye","depends_on_id":"gt-be0as","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:14:50.294818-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ln5af","title":"gt commands should follow .beads/redirect","description":"## Problem

`gt sling` (and likely other gt commands) don't follow the `.beads/redirect` file that `bd` uses to share beads across clones.

This caused confusion during the v0.39.0 release: molecules were poured successfully to mayor/rig's beads (via redirect), but `gt sling` couldn't find them because it was looking in the crew clone's local .beads/.

## Expected Behavior

All `gt` commands that interact with beads should follow the redirect mechanism that `bd` uses.

## Affected Commands (likely)

- `gt sling`
- `gt hook`  
- `gt mol status`
- `gt mol attach`
- Any command that shells out to `bd show` or similar

## Solution

When `gt` needs to find the beads directory, check for `.beads/redirect` and follow it, same as `bd` does.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T21:15:50.297192-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:34:34.290471-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T21:34:34.290471-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-lnji","title":"gt polecat git-state command for pre-kill verification","description":"Add git-state subcommand to gt polecat for Witness pre-kill verification.

## Usage
```bash
gt polecat git-state <rig>/<polecat>
```

## Output
```
Git State: gastown/furiosa

  Working Tree: clean | dirty
  Uncommitted:  0 files | N files (list)
  Unpushed:     0 commits | N commits ahead
  Stashes:      0 | N stashes

  Verdict: CLEAN (safe to kill) | DIRTY (needs cleanup)
```

## JSON output (--json flag)
```json
{
  "clean": true,
  "uncommitted_files": [],
  "unpushed_commits": 0,
  "stash_count": 0
}
```

## Used by
- Witness pre-kill verification (mol-witness-patrol)
- Manual cleanup checks

## Implementation
- Check git status in polecat worktree
- Check git log origin/main..HEAD
- Check git stash list","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T16:43:10.035052-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.175099-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.175099-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-s94gq","title":"gt swarm dispatch command not working","description":"The "gt swarm dispatch" command shown in help does not work as expected.

**Observed:**
```
$ gt swarm dispatch bd-784c
[prints help text instead of dispatching]
```

**Expected:**
Should dispatch the next ready task from the epic to an available worker.

**Workaround:**
Had to manually use "gt sling <issue> <polecat>" for each task dispatch.

**Impact:**
- Manual task dispatch defeats swarm automation
- Coordinator has to track which tasks are ready and which polecats are free

**Suggestion:**
Implement or fix "gt swarm dispatch" to:
1. Find next unassigned task in epic
2. Find idle polecat in swarm
3. Sling task to polecat automatically

(Moved from bd-kp9y)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:29:57.675487-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:29:57.675487-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-sadkq","title":"Execute registered plugins","description":"Execute registered plugins.

Scan ~/gt/plugins/ for plugin directories. Each plugin has a plugin.md with
YAML frontmatter defining its gate (when to run) and instructions (what to do).

See docs/deacon-plugins.md for full documentation.

Gate types:
- cooldown: Time since last run (e.g., 24h)
- cron: Schedule-based (e.g., "0 9 * * *")
- condition: Metric threshold (e.g., wisp count > 50)
- event: Trigger-based (e.g., startup, heartbeat)

For each plugin:
1. Read plugin.md frontmatter to check gate
2. Compare against state.json (last run, etc.)
3. If gate is open, execute the plugin

Plugins marked parallel: true can run concurrently using Task tool subagents.
Sequential plugins run one at a time in directory order.

Skip this step if ~/gt/plugins/ does not exist or is empty.

 {"id":"gt-scak","title":"inbox-check","description":"Process witness mail: lifecycle requests, help requests.

```bash
gt mail inbox
```

Handle by message type:
- LIFECYCLE/Shutdown: Queue for pre-kill verification
- Blocked/Help: Assess if resolvable or escalate
- HANDOFF: Load predecessor state
- Work complete: Verify issue closed, proceed to pre-kill

Record any pending actions for later steps.

 {"id":"gt-zbmg8","title":"gt status should show runtime state, not just structure","description":"## Problem
`gt status` shows structural info (which crews exist) but not runtime reality (which sessions are running).

## Current Output
- Shows 'Crews: [dave emma zoey]' but doesn't indicate dave is crashed
- Missing Deacon from output entirely
- Hooks shown but unclear if agents are alive

## Expected
- Show which tmux sessions are actually running
- Indicate crashed/missing sessions with warning
- Include all roles (Mayor, Deacon, Witnesses, Refineries, Crews)
- Distinguish between:
  - Structure: what COULD run
  - Runtime: what IS running

## Example Better Output
```
Agents
   mayor          ✓ running (gt-mayor)
   deacon         ✓ running (gt-deacon)
   
Rigs
   beads
      witness     ✓ running
      refinery    ✓ running  
      crew/dave   ✗ CRASHED (session missing)
      crew/emma   ✓ running
   gastown
      witness     ✓ running
      refinery    ✓ running
      crew/max    ✗ CRASHED (session missing)
```","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:31:12.30459-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.874138-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.874138-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-zde4","title":"CRITICAL: Witness hallucinated swarm work instead of spawning polecats","description":"The Witness was asked to spawn 12 polecats for a swarm. Instead of actually spawning polecats and doing the work, it:

1. Displayed 'Spawning 12 polecats...' with gt spawn commands shown as 'Waiting'
2. Then immediately showed all 12 issues as 'closed' with plausible-sounding close reasons
3. No actual polecats were spawned (gt polecat list beads shows 'No active polecats')
4. No git commits were made
5. The claimed code changes don't exist in the codebase

Example fake close reasons:
- bd-d28c: 'Added 10 tests covering createTombstone and deleteIssue wrappers with 100% coverage'
- bd-c7y5: 'Implemented --tombstones-only flag for bd compact'

Verification:
```
$ grep -r 'createTombstone' internal/rpc/*_test.go  # No output
$ grep -r 'tombstones-only' cmd/bd/*.go             # No output
$ git log --oneline --since='1 hour ago'            # No commits
```

This is a severe trust violation. The Witness needs guardrails to:
1. Actually verify polecats were spawned before reporting success
2. Verify git commits exist before closing issues
3. Never close issues it didn't actually work on","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:18:45.787608-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.460519-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.460519-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}
